post.content:
Given two time series data lists,

First List
[(1, 3), (3, 1), (5,3), (6, 4), (10, 1)]
Explanation:
What this means is that at timestamp 1 the value is 3, and then at timestamp 3 the value is 1 and so on. 
Note: Missing/Undefined value on any timestamp is extrapolated backwards from the next timestamp. For example, value at timestamp 2 based on the example above would be 1.

Second List
[(2, 3), (6, 3), (9, 2)]

Write a function to aggregate these two time series data into one
Answer
[(1, 6), (2, 4), (3, 4), (5,6), (6, 7), (9, 3), (10,1)]

Solution:
Similar idea to [interval list intersection](https://leetcode.com/problems/interval-list-intersections/) or [merge intervals](https://leetcode.com/problems/merge-intervals/) questions. Linear single pass solution.

Thanks for posting @consolewarn. The example is very helpful.
I was a little confused when you said, "For example, value at timestamp 2 based on the example above would be 1." But I think I get it after looking at the answer.

Anything that has a star is extrapolated from the next timestamp, anything without a star, actually exists in one or both of the lists. So if you added the first list + second list you get the results below:
(1,3) + (1,3)* = (1,6)
(2,1)* + (2,3) = (2,4)
(3,1) + (3,3)* = (3, 4)
timeseries 4, does not exist in either list, so skip
(5,3) + (5,3)* = (5,6)
...

I think this question is more like [#21](https://leetcode.com/problems/merge-two-sorted-lists) Merge Sorted Lists.
this was asked to me today at uber for sse role

I feel this is just like merging two sorted lists. We will pick the smaller head but its value will be updated by adding the bigger value node. If both the nodes are same move head in both the streams.

Time : O(M+N) -> Assuming the lists are sorted.
Space: O(M+N)

I was asked as a follow up question that what you will do if this data was coming as a stream, and I answered that it could be only done by maintaining a buffer because until we have data from both streams to extrapolate backwards we cant get the solution for the current timestamp(assuming we have exhausted all previous timestamps). This seemed to not fit with the interviewer and he took it on his ego when I asked him to suggest a better approach himself.
you can also do this by https://leetcode.com/problems/range-addition/
what level was this interview for? YOE?
